rulings.Case Title: JOHN CHORBA VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITIONER JOHN CHORBA MD MOTION FOR HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law and Motion Calendar for Jan-02-2025, line 1, PETITIONER JOHN CHORBA MD MOTION FOR HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. Off calendar. Both parties failed to provide courtesy copies of the filings including the administrative record in compliance with Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/MEW)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. SRI GANESHA SF RESTAURANT LLC
rulings.Rulings: Matter On the Law and Motion Calendar for Jan-02-2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT. Off calendar per moving party's failure to provide courtesy copies in compliance with Local Rule 2.7B. In addition, there is no proof of service of the demurrer. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/MEW)

rulings.Case Title: RACHEL CROCKETT VS. LLOYD & MOUSILLI, PLLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter On the Law and Motion Calendar for Jan-02-2025, line 3, DEFENDANT LLOYD & MOUSILLI, PLLC DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained as to Plaintiff Rachel Crockett only on the1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th and 6th causes of action with leave to amend whether Crockett worked the majority of time in California and/or had a base of operations in California. (Ward v. United Airlines, Inc. (2020) 9 Cal.5th 832, 741.) Overruled as to 7th and 8th causes of action. Demurrer moot as to a 9th cause of action as so such cause of action is stated in the body of the complaint. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/MEW)
